Park Law Group, P.C., will close on your new property with active liens against it. I have had an active lien on a property for 2 years, and the property was recently closed by Park Law, and they take no responsibility. Not one time did they attempt to reach out to me, showing complete disregard and a lack of care. I called on 5/31/2023 and was promised a call back; they never called. I reached out again on 6/6, and now Park Law Group is putting blame on the title search company that they hired. Michelle suggested that I go after the title search company. Why should I? Park Law was the company that hired this title company, not me. So now the new homeowner, whom they shook hands with, has purchased a new home along with a lien against it! Shame on you, Park Law Group.

Working with the Schaefer Law Group was truly one of the smartest decisions I’ve ever made. From day one, Jennifer Farris and her team went above and beyond—not only showing professionalism and expertise, but also genuine care and empathy for my situation. I had just moved to Georgia from Ohio and felt completely overwhelmed after GEICO refused to work with me, even though their client was clearly at fault. Their final offer to me was only $1,000, and I honestly didn’t know what to do until Jennifer and her team stepped in.

What people need to understand is that when you go through any type of legal situation, patience is key. The process isn’t instant—it takes time to get the outcome you truly deserve. But when you have a transparent, honest, and responsive team like Jennifer’s, you can trust that every step is handled with care and precision. They kept me informed, fought for me every step of the way, and ultimately delivered a result that far exceeded my expectations.

If you’ve experienced any kind of incident, tragedy, or legal issue, I can’t recommend the Schaefer Law Group enough. They don’t just represent you—they stand by you.

After I got rear ended the other guys insurance carrier wanted to drag their feet and we’re going to deny my claim. Called Schaefer Law Group and they handled it and got a settlement within the week and got more than I was expecting. If insurance is trying to jerk you around, call Schaefer!! You’ll be glad you did.
Great legal service!!!!

Christine was very straightforward about the possible outcomes and always seemed to be one step ahead in planning my sons defense strategy. I am profoundly grateful for Christine’s services.

If you find yourself in need and are seeking representation, know that the level of service you will receive at The Koehler Firm with Christine Koehler and Katie Huckaby is guaranteed to exceed your expectations.

Attorney Fredis Romero is an exceptional attorney whose professionalism and dedication are truly commendable. His deep understanding of the law, combined with a sharp strategic mind, consistently leads to outstanding results for his clients. Beyond his legal prowess, Fredis possesses a remarkable character; he is a person of integrity, empathy, and unwavering commitment to justice. His ability to connect with clients on a personal level, offering clear guidance and genuine support, makes him not only a formidable advocate but also a trusted confidant. Attorney Fredis Romero is, without a doubt, the best attorney you can have in your corner.

I had a great experience working with Fredis Romero! He was extremely professional from start to finish and always kept me informed about the status of my case. I never felt left in the dark, and he was consistently available to answer my calls and questions. He made the entire process much less stressful and ultimately secured a very strong settlement for my case. I highly recommend him to anyone looking for a reliable and dedicated personal injury attorney.

If I could give zero stars, I absolutely would. I was completely professional when I first called and left a detailed message about my case. I received a call back from the assistant who assured me he would pass everything along to the attorney.

The next day, I heard nothing. When I called to follow up, I was told, “I didn’t forget — the lawyer just has a lot of cases he’s working with,” and that someone would get back to me. That was the start of five days of me calling every single day, not including the weekend, with no returned calls, no updates, and no courtesy communication at all.

When I finally did receive a call, it was just to tell me that the attorney “thinks he’s not going to be able to take the case.” After a full week of ignoring me, that was the response.

This office showed poor customer service, poor communication, and a complete lack of professionalism. I would not recommend them to anyone looking for a responsive or reliable attorney.
reviews.ownerResponse:
First, I’m truly sorry to hear you felt disappointed with your experience at Romero Law. Based on my recollection, your case involved probation matters in another county, and we needed additional clarification to fully evaluate the situation. It seems there may have been a miscommunication regarding the follow-up information we were waiting on, and I take responsibility for ensuring our team communicates those expectations more clearly moving forward.
I appreciate you considering Romero Law during a difficult time for your family, and I sincerely hope you were able to find the support and guidance you needed.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Ms. Lee-

I am disappointed to read this review, and always eager to address client concerns. My cell phone is (404) 797-1717. All my clients receive it, and all can contact me directly.

I have a starkly different recollection of events, as does staff privy to the call on speakerphone. For those reading this review, all work was completed on this case in 2015, resulting in a successful outcome, wherein the reviewer was awarded primary custody of her children in a contested custody matter. She contacted us one early evening this past summer to write a letter, and then began calling nearly every hour, starting in the early hours of the following morning. (As the review reflects, these events took place over the course of one evening and the following morning, so our firm imposed no delay upon her response time.) By the time I got to my office that morning, I pulled her old file and noticed she had not paid her previous bill, which had not been mailed to a dead address, but had been sent to her e-mail and furnished to her with her final court order at the time her case was resolved. This balance had been ignored for over four years. While we are happy to assist former clients as a courtesy for a letter where it may be helpful, we cannot help those who, despite a successful outcome in their case, elect not to pay their bills. I informed the former client of this policy. The reviewer describes us saying, “We are not collections attorneys.” For clarification, when she asked why we didn’t keep pursuing the bill, we told her that we do not refer our accounts receivable to collection agencies, because we don’t want to harm our client’s credit.

When we declined to help, the reviewer was upset, but informed us that a well-respected lawyer and former judge, known to our firm, would help her, and we wished her well. I continue to wish this client well. I would point readers to our other reviews, as I believe they better reflect the client experience we strive for and deliver as a team each day.

Three years later I went to court and walked out just about a free man. Not only didn’t his way work in the courtroom, but it’s changed my life in every single way you could imagine. He was very stern, but he totally knew what he was talking about. His plan is 100%, it’s just a person has to do the footwork. He’s very professional and his team is on top of everything so you don’t slip through the crack. I am so grateful for meeting this man, I would not be the person I am today clean and sober if I didn’t walk into that office 3 years ago.
reviews.ownerResponse: I was honored to help you work your way through everything. Congratulations on beginning your life anew. Pay it forward!

reviews.text: Kyle and Elicia were beyond fantastic! I never dreamed like most people that I would be faced with being told I want a divorce especially after 15 years years. But they were there to make the process as easy as possible and as civil as possible and keeping the kids 1st. I eventually made it through still sane all thanks to Kyle and his team. I can't say enough great things and another HUGE plus they were beyond reasonable cost wise! They will be my go to for any other needs in the future.
Proffessional
Kyle and Elicia are a great team. I will definitely be returning to them if I need help again.

We had a great experience with attorney Kyle Epps and his team! From the very first phone call I felt confident this was the firm I wanted to use. They are organized, professional, and easy to communicate with. I would highly recommend using this firm.

From my first appointment with Attorney Epps I knew I was at the right place.  By the time I left I felt 100% better and knew he would do a good job. Attorney Epps was a gentleman and a professional throughout my entire time with him.  This is your man if you need his services.  Thank you again Attorney Epps for everything you did for me.

This man is so selfless from the very day we met him he sat with us explained everything and made us not only feel like everything was going to be okay but made sure that it was. He was also working behind the scenes with his many connections to bring us to the best conclusion possible. Would highly recommend attorney Epps and his team for family and friends and we are very glad we decided to stick with him because he gave 100% the entire way and made sure every detail required for maximum success was provided. Amazing service all the way around. Very well connected and informed concerning the ins and outs of criminal cases. Was able to negotiate an amazing deal on our behalf which resulted in 0 jail time. Makes sure all necessary preparation is taken care of and is easy to get in contact with. Would highly recommend Attorney Epps for felony and misdemeanor cases. 10/10

Kyle did a great job on my daughter’s case and we are very thankful.  Things would have been much different had we not had him as our attorney.  Communicating with his office is so easy.  They have a client portal that you can log into to see appointments, documents and messages.  Elicia, his paralegal, is a pleasure to deal with as well.  Highly recommend Kyle.

Let me be very clear that there is ZERO embellishment nor exaggeration when I say that I was facing a doom I couldn't even comprehend when I contacted Kyle. I had a misdemeanor that turned into a felony through no fault of my own, other than believing a local officer when I shouldn't have.

Kyle went out of his way from the very start, before any discussion of payment even. He sat me down, reassured me, and laid out the plan exactly how it was going to work. Not one thing ever happened without Kyle or Elicia notifying me immediately.

And when I say they handled everything, I mean EVERYTHING. I spent a grand total of 20 minutes in a courtroom, and 15 of that was being there early. What I thought was going to be a year or so in jail and thousands in fines turned into 3 months in the pre-trial diversion  diversion program, 20 hours of community service.

Furthermore, his office is very up to date using a client portal that sent documents, updates, and appointments directly to my phone.

I wasted far too much time calling other lawyers, and praying I was poor enough to afford the public defender. Don't make my mistake. Skip the other lawyers, skip the panic, and go directly to contacting Kyle. He had my back from the start, and was more than worth every penny!

Kyle helped me with a Family Law matter and also with having a stolen car returned.  He exceeded my expectations at every point.  He was always available, thoughtful, knowledgeable, courteous and matter of fact.  From the beginning, I always felt he had my back. If any of my friends, family or acquaintances ever need an attorney, I will recommend Kyle.

Mr. Epps handled my case with relative ease, I feel like I got sound practical advice. I’m certain his depth of knowledge saved a substantial amount of money and shortened the process. I hope I don’t need an attorney again but if I do Kyle will be my first call.

Mr. Epps is a man of few words, but those that he uses carry some weight.    As an attorney, he was well rounded, knowledgeable, funny and reassuring.   He returned phone calls and e-mails and his flat fee system should be used by ALL criminal defense attorneys.

If I am mistaken and this firm is not for Melvin Raines II please let me know and I will happily delete. Yari Lawson is a wonderful attorney and I hope he has not joined firms with Melvin Raines because it would be a tragedy.

DO NOT hire Melvin Raines as an attorney. I am not his client but I have been with his client as she has tried relentlessly to reach him to discuss her case for over a year. At one point she called him daily without success. Melvin Raines agreed to represent her in her divorce (and was paid a retainer) yet her case was dismissed earlier this month because after 5 YEARS Melvin Raines had only attempted to serve her husband ONCE. Even the judge told her she needs to find another lawyer. If he didn’t want to represent her he should have removed himself from the case.

Melvin is the only attorney I would ever hire for violation of probation in Bibb County. Reasonable isn't a strong enough word price wise. Prosecution tried to fry every other defendant with better records than my own, explaining why said defendant shouldn't be granted bond or have hold lifted etc. when it came to Me their only words were "no opposition". Melvin Is and has been on fire with violations for as long as I've known his name, which has been for years.

I really appreciated Mr. Lawson's persistence and constant communication about the progress of my case.  He helped me make a sound decision that was best for my family and me.  I highly recommend Lawson Raines, LLP to anyone in need of great legal representation.

Ryan Bramblett is by far one of the best attorneys in Georgia he was able to get the best possible outcome for me in my court case despite being officially contracted as my attorney for the case only one day before court. I retained Mr. Bramblett in 2022 after my daughter’s initial abuse disclosures, paying him from my own savings as a stay-at-home mother. I trusted that with the evidence we had, the matter would be handled promptly. Instead, the judge directed him at our first hearing to file a motion to reconsider, but he never did. That failure, along with multiple mishandled filings and inaccurate affidavits of service, caused my case to be dismissed and forced us into years of ongoing litigation.

Because of these errors, I was left to refile, retain new counsel, and continue litigating the same issues that should have been resolved long ago. The financial burden has been devastating, I exhausted my savings, maxed out credit cards, and ultimately had to liquidate our family home and cars just to keep up with the mounting legal costs.

This was not simply a matter of strategy differences; it was a pattern of neglect and incompetence that deeply harmed my case and family. I would strongly caution anyone considering him for sensitive custody or abuse-related matters. #JusticeForMilan

I closed my  mortgage refinance with this law firm and they took a very nonchalant approach to the entire process. Initially I did not think it was a big deal but I was severely mistaken. They failed to address or complete multiple steps during the process for something the law firm should be well equipped to handle.

1) I had to go out of my way chasing someone down to get wiring instructions so the loan would close on time.
2) During the signing, the process was discombobulated (the closing packet/key documents were not printed prior to arrival, the closing costs were doubled without any heads up, and they process took entirely too long).

3) Then the kicker, I get an email saying “(we) handled the refinance of your home, but missed having one document signed. Currently we have (your wife) on title, but she did not sign any of our documents. We will need to remove her from title to comply with the lender’s title documents. Please advise a good location, date and time that she can swing by our Duluth office to sign the document. Please advise asap since we are unable to finalize your refinance closing without this. Thank you!”

Yeah no big deal, your wife is not on the title and your refinance is not completed. Also how do they want me to respond? Your Welcome! Thanks for ruining the whole process!!!

I had a great experience with the Barnwell Law Group. I was guided through an emotional divorce with grace and clarity; efficient and always responsive which gave me peace of mind at every step of the process, all at an affordable price. Divorce resolved absent a contested process which greatly benefited myself and ex-spouse financially as well as emotionally.

I am incredibly grateful for Cory’s help and representation. I initially had a judgment entered against me due to improper responses while I was unrepresented. I was not given a court date, and the ruling was made in favor of the other party, which put me at a serious disadvantage.

Cory successfully helped me get a judgment reversed in Superior Court. With very little time left, he agreed to take my case as a last-minute hire and acted quickly and effectively.

From day one, Cory was attentive, responsive, and professional. He took the time to fully understand my situation, recognized the urgency, and acted quickly.

Cory is extremely knowledgeable and explained everything clearly, making sure I understood the process and my options. Despite the time constraints, he worked diligently and successfully got the judgment reversed, giving me a fair opportunity that I was previously denied.

His fees were reasonable, especially considering the level of dedication, skill, and urgency he brought to my case. Throughout my case, Cory demonstrated strong attention to detail, responsiveness, and a clear understanding of the urgency involved.

I highly recommend Cory to anyone who needs strong legal representation, especially if time is critical. I am very appreciative of everything he did for me!!

I’ve had the pleasure of working with Cory Barnwell at Barnwell Law Group on several legal matters for my company and personal.  Cory was professional, knowledgeable, and approachable throughout the entire process.  He consistently responded to emails and phone calls promptly, making communication smooth and stress free.  His expertise and guidance were invaluable, and he achieved excellent results in resolving each of my legal issues. reviews.text: Worst closing I've ever had.  Buyer never given details to wire funds until day of closing.  Real Estate Agent was never told of closing time nor was I.  I had to call to get the closing time.  The closing statement left off the agent commission so that had to be corrected.  So buyer funds were delayed and then when they got the buyer funds - 3 days later, i was told agent never sent earnest money so I had to wait for that to happen.

If you could mess up any part of a closing, they did. Every part of our closing with them went poorly from start to finish. We received closing documents at ten minutes before closing time on the business day before our closing and they were incorrect. We spent an hour waiting for correct numbers at the table. Wire transfers were requested 10+ times and one still didn't make it on that business day. They pushed the closing and made us wait a weekend for a new closing date and time. This closing office was about as slow and unprofessional as they come. I highly recommend choosing another closing attorney.

worst closer in my experience. They never reviewed or corrected the CD before closing and changed the numbers couple times at the closing tables. Could close the same day because of them. made some changes with the numbers at the closing table which we didn't hear from them before and we had no options to get the numbers work for us at that moment. Never replied or read the emails carefully from us or from the lender. Charges high and poor service. I guess they take more responsibility than they are capable of. My agent chose them abut I won't recommend.

DO NOT USE THIS TITLE COMPANY! The numbers were not right twice! I closed in June. Reached out in August about not receiving my deed. I was told due to COVID give it a few more weeks. I reached out Nov 9th and the receptionist stated it was delivered in Aug. I asked where did you deliver it? IT WAS DELIVERED TO A EMPTY LOT!! I could not believe it. This company is horrible. I closed my  mortgage refinance with this law firm and they took a very nonchalant approach to the entire process. Initially I did not think it was a big deal but I was severely mistaken. They failed to address or complete multiple steps during the process for something the law firm should be well equipped to handle.

1) I had to go out of my way chasing someone down to get wiring instructions so the loan would close on time.
2) During the signing, the process was discombobulated (the closing packet/key documents were not printed prior to arrival, the closing costs were doubled without any heads up, and they process took entirely too long).

3) Then the kicker, I get an email saying “(we) handled the refinance of your home, but missed having one document signed. Currently we have (your wife) on title, but she did not sign any of our documents. We will need to remove her from title to comply with the lender’s title documents. Please advise a good location, date and time that she can swing by our Duluth office to sign the document. Please advise asap since we are unable to finalize your refinance closing without this. Thank you!”

Yeah no big deal, your wife is not on the title and your refinance is not completed. Also how do they want me to respond? Your Welcome! Thanks for ruining the whole process!!!

I brought a case from another law firm to Alexa in 2021. She was shocked at the story I had to tell in terms of issues with my ex around parenting time, contempt and child support. There were many hours of preparation that went into our first court date only to have the judge decide not to listen to our case and force counseling. About a year later we went back to court and Alexa and her team spent hours again on prep work, deciding they could use little of what they'd charged me to prep before.
The judge was absolutely horrible to deal with and after two years, decided to not listen to our case as she had other things to do and wanted us to work it out. After hearing that information, I went back into the room with Alexa and she told me "I never would have filed this case in the first place". After charging me over $30K in legal fees across two years, she decides to tell me I don't have a case? I find it highly unethical to charge a client that much money and wait until you lost again to tell them you wouldn't have filed the case.

reviews.text: I didn't know what to expect when I reached out to Shewmaker & Lewis LLC for a lawyer, but I knew I needed help. A work situation blew up out of proportion. I am glad my help came from Mrs. Lewis. Right away I could tell that she was the straight to business, no fluff kind of help that I needed. My case was complex and new for both sides of the situation. Mrs. Lewis took time to dive into the literature and my circumstances to build my side of the case in a way that was acknowledging to the opposing side but unapologetically illuminating of the facts and details to support my perspective and the foul play I was experiencing at work. She amplified my voice when I had to walk a fine line in a merky situation. We won my case by unanimous vote, completely shifting perspectives of the opposition, leading me to outcomes that support my professional development in the future. From start to finish, Mrs. Lewis was was caring, kind, professional, and organized. She was a great colleague and friend to have in this process. I was advised by many on the opposing side that she was a good lawyer and that I should keep her contact information. That's exactly what I intend to do. I intend to share her information with anyone who needs it in the future.

I contacted the VA seeking legal assistance and was directed to Mr. Shewmaker. He has provided me with the best legal assistance I could have hoped for. He is blunt, honest, and will fight for you. Thank you Steve for your assistance.

Attorney Washburn is a very stand-up guy. I had a 19 year old ticket outstanding, and I needed to get it settled. I live out of state, and figured it would be easier to find an attorney to handle it. The first attorney I contacted wanted to charge me $1000 to go to court, and I would still have to pay the fine. I called attorney Washburn, and he advised me that he would make a phone call and call me back. He called me back and told me he would email me the link so I could simply pay the fine. His fee for that? Nothing. He could have taken asked for payment for the 10 minutes it took to get the information, but he didn't, and I really appreciate it. I'm in the industry of providing a service to customers, too, and I often will simply do things out of the kindness of my heart. It's really nice to know that there are still some compassionate people in this world. Thank you, attorney Washburn!!

reviews.ownerResponse:
Marcos, your case had extremely bad charges, facts, and evidence. Despite the charges, facts, and evidence, we successfully negotiated a deal saving you from a year or more in prison and multiple additional years of probation. We did our duties to protect your rights and protect you from much steeper criminal punishment. Further, the case has been discharged resulting in no conviction on your criminal history.
Immigration consequences for any criminal case should be thoroughly discussed with your retained immigration attorney. You had ample time to have your immigration attorney review your case for adverse consequences and we encouraged you to do so.
Feel free to give us a call to discuss how we can further assist you or provide guidance to help in your immigration case.

I won’t accuse—only provide facts. This attorney will promise you the world, especially if it involves attorney malpractice. In actuality it is a bait/switch. The reality is he only wants a retainer and likely depends on it and nothing more. Even if there is a contingency he will attach a lien to some obscure part hoping you will sign and forget about it.

He assured, as others have stated, there was a proverbial slam-dunk attorney malpractice and believe me there was…he just completely tucked-tail. He actually witnessed two (2) attorneys not only lie repeatedly in Court but one also commit or possibly commit suborned perjury (attempted to coerce a witness he represented into committing perjury with false records). This attorney never called an opposing witness and literally stood and watched as a judge takes over $50K from his client without him objecting or saying a word. His client is a highly-decorated and severely disabled Combat Veteran.

Post-hearing he blames the client for “misunderstanding” the offer he made for contingency which is completely made-up and even after seeing his own email stating this he ignores it. He sends a billing invoice claiming to have racked-up a bill twice the retainer amount with names of office staff you never heard of once.

If this attorney witnessed/observes perjury or other attorney misconduct in Court as others have stated he has an ethical responsibility to report it to the State Bar and possibly to police as well depending on the severity—why would an attorney that supposedly specializes in this area of holding attorneys accountable not be suing them?

I would encourage anyone to do a deep-dive into the number of business/LLCs/Shell Companies listed under multiple names, assumed names, etc. Most if not all attach to 265 S Culver that looks like a house in a residential neighborhood with no less than four (4) law firms and about 25-30 supposed companies…including MegaChurch and Jackalope Holdings. Wish I could make this up.

There are certain games you should always avoid at the County Fair or street corner in NYC-you are never intended to win this hustle.

** Update—even before realizing we were going to be left waiting/hoping we still needed an attorney to appeal and actually thought I could convince this person to do what they contracted to do as an attorney and paid which was represent and then drop the hammer on some crooked attorneys. What we faced was an appeal for what he failed to fight for in Court. There was brief communication asking for a price to file an appeal—$3500 without hesitation from him.

When the subject was brought up about the opposing attorneys lying in Court (all transcribed) was told “that is beyond a civil matter.” Really? This is all the more reason you should be suing them like you advertised and agreed to. The appeal we filed for ZERO dollars was the Superior Court judge recognizing criminal actions meaning this attorney, again, never had any intention of suing anyone despite how egregious the other side’s behavior was and remains and then states “he wants to part ways and focus on other things.” Tread Carefully, there are snakes everywhere,

Believe the negative reviews about Atty. Ney they are all probably true as many of them mirror my experience with him. He took me for 1000$ fee on a case that was "clear malpractice" his words.... then he bailed on us when opposing atty fought back ...YOU HAVE BEEN WARNED ! This guy is a fraud !

Please take a moment to review other complaints about him online. We engaged Mr. Ney to handle a conflict of interest case. Through email, phone calls, and face-to-face meetings, he assured us that our former attorney was not entitled to any money. After providing him with $1000.00 for a case study, he returned confirming his acceptance of our case and reiterated that our ex-attorney had no claim to funds. Consequently, we ceased negotiations on a $25000 settlement with the ex-attorney. Upon receiving the remaining flat fee of $7000.00, he stopped communication. we went to his office he did not show uo. Despite assurances of a jury trial and the use of expert witnesses, none materialized – these promises proved false. Furthermore, he neglected to inform us of the hearing date, attending alone, resulting in the case being lost. As a consequence, we now find ourselves obligated to pay the ex-attorney $57000.00. Once he gets the money he is gone. I filed a complaint with Bar hoping they discipline him, then I will sue him.
I was very fortunate to find Jacob, who has been true to his word and very professional during a difficult time for me. I recommend him to my colleagues.

reviews.text: read the contract before you sine it or he’ll do you dirty he also forced me to get Epidural in my neck claiming that if I don’t get the shots the insurance won’t cover any medical experience he’ll tell you what every you want to hear to get you to sine a contract I didn’t read the the contract and he took 100% advantage of me don’t trust this guy. reviews.ownerResponse:
Kamish, you had 3 cases with us over the last 4 years and we never would force anyone to get medical treatment and you know this from your 3 cases with us. For anyone seeing this review, we always tell our clients that if you’re hurt in an accident, go seek medical care, because if you do not seek medical care, the insurance company has nothing to consider for your case. In an email to us, Kamish stated, “you said that if I do not get treatment they won’t pay for any of the treatment.” Our response to Kamish was, “EXACTLY!” In no way did we force this client to go to treatment. The attorneys have over 40+ years of experience amongst them, and we do not force our clients to do anything they do not want to do. Kamish further states to read the contract and we always advise that you should read any contract. We do not hide or mislead anyone with our contracts and to be perfectly open on the topic, our contract states exactly this, “We work on a contingency basis meaning we get paid 33% of the settlement if we can resolve your case without a lawsuit and 40% if we go to a lawsuit. IF WE DO NOT WIN, YOU DO NOT OWE US ANYTHING.” We don’t know what Kamish is referring to, but our contracts are clear and we do not need to take advantage of any clients. If Kamish was so worried about our contracts and advice, why did he hire us on 3 separate occasions! In all seriousness, not all cases are winners and we don’t think it’s fair to blame the lawyers if the case does not resolve favorably. We fight hard for our clients, whoever they may be.

Sincerely and truthfully,

Cuadra & Patel.

I’ve had professional experiences with other O’Kelly & Sorohan offices, but the Duluth branch is a different story. My experience highlights three significant areas where this office falls short of Georgia’s legal and ethical standards:

Breach of Confidentiality (Rule 1.6): Look at how this office responds to negative reviews. They frequently disclose specific customer information to publicly "attack" or defend themselves. Under Georgia State Bar Rule 1.6, attorneys are prohibited from revealing information relating to the representation of a client. Using a public forum to air client details is an ethical red flag.

Lack of Communication (Rule 1.4): We have been dealing with an Earnest Money dispute for months. Raissa Rolim has been consistently unresponsive. When she does reply, it’s a vague "I have to check with a supervisor," followed by weeks of silence. Georgia Rule of Professional Conduct 1.4 requires lawyers to keep clients reasonably informed and promptly comply with requests for information. This office has done neither.

Withholding Cost Information (O.C.G.A. § 23-3-90): We have repeatedly asked for the specific costs to file an interpleader action so the buyer and seller can reach a settlement. This request has been ignored for months. Under O.C.G.A. § 23-3-90, the holder of funds is supposed to act as a neutral stakeholder. By withholding the cost of the action, the firm is effectively blocking the parties from making an informed decision to settle, unnecessarily dragging out the dispute.

If you value transparency, privacy, and basic professional responsiveness, I would strongly suggest using a different firm.

reviews.text: They did our closing.  We were the sellers.  They added some fees we had to pay and it was done in a shady way.   I would never trust this firm to work with.  Run.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Hi Patrick,

Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. We appreciate your review and would like to assure you that we take all feedback seriously. We are sorry to hear that you believe we added certain fees for you to pay at closing and that those fees were added in a shady way. We are also sorry to hear that you feel as if you cannot trust our law firm. The fees that you are referring to were Homeowner Association’s fees which included an HOA initiation fee and account set up fee. While these two fees are typically paid by the buyer at closing, unfortunately, you failed to disclose these two fees to the buyer on the community association disclosure. The community association disclosure is an exhibit that was made a part of the purchase and sale agreement that you signed. Since you did not accurately disclosed these two fees to the buyer on the community association disclosure, the fees became seller paid fees which is why they were added for you to pay at closing. Please feel free to reach out to us if there is anything else you would like to discuss. Thank you.

reviews.text: I had a wonderful experience with O'Kelley and Sorohan Law Associates.  It was timely. The communication was great, and overall, it was a great experience. I would close with them again.

They handled the closing on my house.  We were scheduled for a 1pm close.  I spoke to the pre-closer at noon and she indicated they did not yet have a clear to close from the bank.  I was told to shoot for 3pm.  I was on 316 and spoke to the pre-closer again and she indicated we would need to reschedule for the next day and we were 100% NOT going to close.  My agent said to go to the office anyway.  We ended up closing at 5:30pm.  Not sure where their miscommunication is...  They did have complimentary candy and bottled water.  The attorney was efficient.  Just overall a confusing and mentally taxing experience.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for taking the time to provide feedback about your recent experience with our law firm. We appreciate your honesty and apologize for any confusion or inconvenience you may have experienced during the closing process of your house.

We understand that effective communication is crucial to ensuring a smooth and stress-free closing, and we apologize for any miscommunications on our part. We strive to provide clear and timely updates, as we receive information from the lender, to our customers throughout the closing process. As you indicated in your review, we did not have the clear to close from your lender to be able to close at the original scheduled time. It’s our understanding that your agent told you to come to our office regardless and we closed your transaction after hours when we received the required lender approval. Had we been closing within regular business hours we would have closed the following business day as communicated by our precloser.

Should you have any further concerns or if there is anything else we can do to address your experience, please do not hesitate to reach out to us directly. We value your business and are committed to ensuring your satisfaction.

reviews.text: If i could give them zero stars i would, our company has used them previously for the past years for multiple transactions around North GA and never had an issue until we sent them over our final transaction with them. They informed us they were familiar with novations and understood the process which they did not, they were very unresponsive and last minute with everything to ending up finding out they canceled our transaction same day of closing and then closed on the home without our company, We had several team members reaching out to Lindsay and was informed that they were not going to close the transaction but we ended up founding out they removed us off the deal and closed the transaction with all other parties. WE DO NOT RECOMMONED IF YOU DO NOVATIONS OR ASSIGNMENTS. Our company is doing 30-40 transactions a month as assignments and novations and we will take our clients elsewhere!!!!!
reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear Mr. Soliz,
Thank you for your review. We are sorry you feel this way. Our law firm is familiar with novations, assignments, investor closings, and all of the variations thereof, and we do understand the process for closing these types of transactions as we successfully close many of them, however, we do require that all paperwork and documents are executed properly by the appropriate parties involved in each transaction. We are unable to accept novations, assignments and contracts that are not executed properly and where we are unable to contact the parties involved in the transaction. Further, we make sure to follow title insurance guidelines. Your paperwork did not line up and it is hard to close a transaction when we reach out to the seller and they do not know that the transaction is happening. We decline to close transactions where the paperwork is not properly executed and where we are unable to talk to all parties in involved in the transaction. We cannot control if the parties choose to go to a different closing attorney for a particular transaction. Please feel free to reach out directly to us and we are happy to discuss further. Thank you, and again, apologies for the experience not meeting your expectations.

reviews.text:
If you are a real estate investor DO NOT use this firm! If you like POOR communication in which people involved
In your transaction likes to answer calls or emails. This is the place for you. Time and communication is priority in real estate and my time has been COMPLETELY waisted. I repeat if poor service and communication is your goal then you found the right company. Nobody will answer my call or emails at the Tucker Location. Carlton and pre closer Smalls have not responded to my emails and calls in 5 days and I’m the BUYER! Super unprofessional and highly disappointing. Investors and investor agents try somewhere else. reviews.ownerResponse:
Hi, We’re sorry that you feel that there was poor communication from us on this file, but we’d would like to clarify a few things. We believe you are misdirecting your frustration on us instead of acknowledging the timelines in the contract and who’s responsible for making sure they are followed through. As closing attorney, we do our best to provide everyone involved with the best possible experience and customer service from initial title order to the closing table. We’re more than happy to interpret the contract, but do not negotiate or write the agreement between the parties. Also, when agents are involved, we communicate through them since they represent you in the transaction.

On Monday January 8th, after your due diligence period ended, we received an email that you did not want to move forward with the closing. That day, the Listing Agent notified the Selling Agent (your agent) via email that Holder would make a determination of the EMD in case of a termination. You sent a separate email to us asking to refund the EMD, and we responded with our initial thoughts and that there was no termination as of that date. We copied your agent and the Listing Agent, so that all the necessary parties were in the loop. We indicated that we could issue a 10-day letter if there was a dispute over the earnest money, but we had not yet received a termination with proposed disbursement of earnest money. From our initial response on the 8th through the contact closing date on the 12th and as of this date, we still have not received a termination with a proposal for disbursement. We suggest that you reach out to your agent to discuss the next steps.

reviews.ownerResponse: Hi Nathan, thank you for your review. We would like to have the opportunity to look into your file in more detail if you would be willing to discuss with us and to provide your file information. With what you describe it is likely that the tax bill for 2022 was not yet out at the time of your closing, which would result in tax proration being calculated based off of the 2021 tax bill. Parties agree in most contracts and by signing the acknowledgement page of the settlement statement at the time of closing to adjust prorations after closing if the current year's tax bill is not out at the time of closing. With taxes going up for many in 2022, the adjustment of prorations has been a frequent occurrence. We are happy to help facilitate the adjustment between the parties.

reviews.text:
A deep thanks to Erin Mills, Steven Mills and all who helped battle on our behalf at Mills & Hoopes. We cannot thank this team enough for the sacrifices they made to help us in 2024 & 2025.
Here is to a fantastic and blessed 2026 🙏
Steve is awesome !!

Zach is the best Georgia HOA attorney. I had the pleasure of working with him when our community faced a complex HOA dispute involving fines, a modification request form, and contentious assessments. From our first consultation, Zach explained every legal detail in clear, accessible language, which made a daunting process much more manageable. His deep understanding of HOA regulations, combined with his proactive and results-driven approach, ensured that our issues were resolved efficiently and fairly. Whether you’re grappling with excessive fines, need assistance with a modification request form, or are challenged by unfair assessments, Zach’s expertise is second to none. I highly recommend his services for anyone seeking reliable legal support in all HOA-related matters.

What’s the difference between a vampire and a lawyer? Law School. If that’s true what they say, then the barristers at Mills & Hoopes are less like the loathsome, pestiferous Count Dracula and closer to the Count from Sesame Street; they are friendly, great with numbers, and a helpful part of the community. Some of the lawyers here should start being luggage bodyguards on account of how rarely they lose a case! I like to say that Steve Mills, Scott Hoopes, and the rest of these lawyers are “on the rocks”, because all they care about is just-ice! I might as well be Abraham Lincoln with these guys, because they time and time again prove that I’m in-a-cent!
Best lawyers in town!
Amazing service. Tim specifically has consistently been a constant help whenever I’ve needed legal advice or support. Ask for Tim for excellent quality legal service or litigation.
I called 4 time and left messages. HE DID NOT RETURN MY PHONE CALL 😒 😐

I worked with Eckhart in past and also recently contacted them for help as well. Very helpful and free consultation with all answers to my questions. I gave my name and number and he called back and setup appointment. Highly recommend him.

I hired Scott Hoopes to do a job for me. I paid him monies as requested and he did not even attend hearing that was scheduled.  He has lied and has taken my money without doing  comparable work.  When I inquired of him for status of my case, he decided to send me a long letter and recuse himself without more explanations.  Be careful of the attorney you hire!!!
Great service. Proven results!

I had tried setting up an appointment with them, calling them and speaking with the receptionist. I gave my name, number and the issue to the receptionist three different times and was told I will receive a call from them. I never received any call from them. I don’t think they are reliable. I needed some urgent advice and they kept me waiting for so many days before I decided to move on with some other firm. I will never approach them again.
Never returns phone calls or emails - referred by a friend but can’t depend on service.

If I could give 0 stars I would give them 0 stars. On February 2, 2025, I submitted documents to case manager Robert Kwon regarding my citations for driving with a suspended license and excessive window tint. Mr. Kwon informed me that the attorney's services would cost $1,300.00 to handle my case. However, no contract was provided outlining the scope of the law firm's services nor was I informed I would be billed on an hourly basis. After taking my fees, the office did not explain what they would do to help my case. As my court date was approaching for my ticket, I followed up with Mr. Kwon on February 05, 2025 to check the status of my case but received no clear updates. Due to the lack of communication and progress, I requested a refund on February 16, 2025. Mr. Kwon refused, prompting me to inform him that I would report the matter to the Georgia State Bar.
Shortly after, attorney Kennon Peebles, Jr. contacted me, offering to refund 50% of the $1,300.00 on the condition that I sign an agreement not to "disparage" their law firm. I then requested a breakdown of the fees justifying why the full amount was not being refunded. Mr. Peebles provided an invoice stating that attorney services were billed at $250.00 per hour while non-attorney staff work was billed at $125.00 per hour. Upon reviewing the invoice, I noticed several questionable charges. Fore example, I was billed for 2.9 hours of communication with case manager Mr. Kwon, despite our interactions being brief and primary via text, consisting mostly of me requesting case updates. I am certain our
communication did not amount to 2.9 hours of billable time, raising concerns about the firm's billing practices and ethical standards. On February 21, 2025, I gave the law office a final opportunity to return my money in exchange for not reporting the issue to the State Bar of Georgia. They refused to issue a refund.

EVERYONE DO NOT USE THIS FIRM’S SERVICES! THEY ARE UNPROFESSIONAL AND PREDATORY.

Don't waste time or money . This Law Firm is NOT capable to win your case. There will be no updates on your case unless the Law Firm need the payment from you . I am contacting State Bar of GA regarding this Business .

Mr. Peebles really helped not only myself,  but also quite a few of my associates. Great consultations also.
I  Highly reccomend
My lawyer
Don't waste your time and money!!. You been warned.
do not waste time and money with kennon peebles .
It's a nice place for get the results.
Fraud

I have got an accident on August 2014, and the case finally ended on April 2017. If you are patient enough and have a lot of time, go ahead and discuss with them.

I was hit by dui driver, which can be considered as a how easy to win and earn money, however I still cannot understand how they could not make me enough money and they cannot really discuss with the hospital for my medical payment. As a result, they could not even make money they promised me whan the case began.

I really am regreting that I have met him, the assistant, Robert Kwon, in my church for the first time in my life I got an severe accident like this.

I will start by going straight to the point.  If you’re loaded with too much money and if your dream is to be incarcerated for a minor misdemeanor charge then you should definitely retain this law firm.  Not only do you get to spend a lot of money you get to be treated in a most condescending manner by the “Office Administrator” and also get to vacation in a real jail.

I’m going to write in 2 parts, 1 for the attorney Kennon Peebles, and 1 for the “Office Administrator” Robert Kwon.  If you don’t have much time nor the patience to read my whole review, I recommend just going straight to the 2nd part regarding the “Office Administrator” Robert Kwon.

First, the Attorney Peebles :
He met the prosecutor/solicitor for the first time at the court hearing and was told that the solicitor is seeking a real jail time which he responded by just postponing the court hearing date for 1 month later.  Although he had additional 1 month to prepare for the postponed court hearing he again showed up without any preparation.  And obviously he was told the same thing about seeking real jail time by the solicitor so he asked for the postponement again.  Now 2nd postponement and his confidence of claiming that he will get my case dismissed seemed to be all gone. In our first meeting, Peebles strongly claimed that he can and will get my misdemeanor charge be dismissed.  The most disappointing thing was his action of trying to make a deal with me instead of making a deal or at least talking with the prosecuting office, he asked what if jail time was half of a month, how many days I would say OK to, etc., What kind attorney did like that!

Secondly, the “Office Administrator” Robert Kwon :
The incompetence of the attorney Peebles is not as big of a problem compared to his office administrator, Robert Kwon’s attitude and demeanor.  As mentioned in other reviews, the way he treats his clients is worse than you can imagine.  At first he will make you think he might be an associate attorney, but you will find out that he’s just an office administrator  only after you look at his business card.   As soon as you engage in few conversations regarding your case it becomes mystery how some one with such impolite, actually “bitchy” attitude can work as an office administrator.  The pretentious and condescending way of talking to you is one thing, to a question if he will come to the court (keep in mind I’ve basically talked to him mostly and talked to Peebles only once, and didn’t quiet understand his position, etc,) he said he comes if I pay separate extra fee for him and said other clients usually do so.  According to what he said, he probably earns much more than the Attorney Peebles.  After the 1st hearing when the 1st postponement was made I’ve even paid more (stupid me thought more money meant they will pay more attention to my case), but I’ve realized I just couldn’t rely on their office when I didn’t get any call backs till just 1 day prior to the actual hearing date.  How can he be only available on the phone when payment was being made then for nearly a month till the hearing date no contact was feasible what so ever.  So on the 1st postponed hearing date, when Peebles got 2nd postponement, as soon as I got out of the Court, I’ve fired him and hired another attorney.
My new attorney got in contact with the prosecuting office within 4 days and my case got officially dismissed.  If you need an attorney just google, you can just google search a “super lawyer”.  The amount of money you would spend in using a “super lawyer” is same as how much you would end up spending, if you retain a law office of Kennon Peebles.  Incompetence for that much amount of money is fraud!

First time I have ever been thoroughly disgusted with the performance of any "professional". Save your time, money and stress and please either consult a real lawyer or if money is a problem, you are much better off handling whichever case you may have yourself.
I also just want to add I have never gone out of my way to write a bad review.

This is an ambulance chasing type law office. They try to milk money from insurance company and those insured with uncreditable law suits thats.  This "lawyer" Keenon, came on my property without a warrant demanding to speak with my son who was involved in an car accident that resulted in his client admitting to the police officer that there was no damage to their car, that their was no injury to any one in the car, and refused any medical treatment.  Now more than 6 months later they are trying to sue my son and the insurance company for damages to their car, for medical treatment that they claim were from the accident.  The facts of the claim are false, the accident was a result of their car being bumped from behind from a car rolling less than 5 mph from a turning lane at a light when the client stopped.  The fact is that the client is a family member of the Keenon's partner and he is just out for money not for what is right or wrong when their client is clearly fabricating the accident because this law office has made up a new story.  Stay clear of this ambulance chasing law office.  State Farm attorney's are going to have a field day with this one when they get to court, if it ever gets to court.

reviews.text: DON’T WASTE YOUR MONEY!!! If I could use a negative star I would. She is unprofessional & rude. She is all about her money & not you as a client. She is as mediocre as they come! She TALKS a good game but there is no ACTION to it! She represented my mother for her divorce & my father walked in with an accident attorney & WON!! After 30+ years of marriage my mom walked away with nothing. She has yet to return emails or phone calls & throughout the trial she was unavailable! SHE IS DISAPPOINTING AS AN ATTORNEY!! YOU CAN DO BETTER & YOU SHOULD!! DON’T WASTE YOUR TIME, ENERGY, OR MONEY ON HER!!!
reviews.ownerResponse:
Ms. Knox, I have never represented you, never spoken to you, and I do not know you, nor have I been disrespectful or rude to you. You alleged that I represented your Mother in a divorce. I am not at liberty to disclose who my clients are or may have been, especially on this platform. However, I will say, if I did represent your Mother, I can assure you she never told me she was dissatisfied with my services and never once complained that her calls or emails were not returned. I would know firsthand, and this is not the scenario. While I do appreciate your interest, this forum is not the place to discuss a case and publicly air someone’s case, especially since you were not involved, in any way.

I along with my staff take great pride in returning calls and emails. Most clients have access to either my cell phone or my paralegal’s cell phone and we pride ourselves in being available to clients and returning calls and emails. We take extra step and make ourselves available to our clients during those emergency situations. We have on several occasions received calls even at 2am in the morning, on weekends, and holidays. We are a small firm; however, we are more personable and fight extremely hard for all of our clients. I go into Court with personal conviction and go to war for my clients every single time. Like most, I earn my living by working hard for my clients. Your statement about me being all about the money is wholly untrue. I rarely charge a trial retainer and I do not bill my clients throughout the case, which is rare in Family Law. I hate having to defend my practice with someone who was never a client and has never spoken with me. My passion is helping people and I do it well. My goal is to win and make the process as seamless as I possibly can. I have the reputation that confirms that, both in and outside of the Courtroom. Do I win every case, NO, but I give it all I have. I give my client’s honesty. I along with my supportive staff work as a team. Because of my team, I have received back-to-back recognition for Georgia Super Lawyers for many years. I am passionate about all of my clients, and I strive to win.

I wish you the best Ms. Knox

reviews.text: Attorney Lin provided me with professional legal services, allowing me to complete my case faster than I had imagined. He himself is also very friendly and trustworthy. The receptionists were also very patient and answered all my questions. It's a professional and humane law firm!

Going to be honest, I did not feel like I got the service like the other reviews have stated here.
I was going through a high conflict divorce case.
At first he was very sympathetic to my case so I felt he would help guide me through the process of the divorce well.

However as I went through the motions, I realized he never really had a plan for my case and was always reactive to the opposing attorney. He even forgot important dates and had to clarify with the judge. It was disheartening to see that. It was night and day compared to the opposing attorney that seemed to have her ducks in a row.

And when I received agressive demands from the opposing attorney, his response would be along the lines like “hes not happy with how the opposing attorney is handling the case”. Which was of no relief to me as he did not seem to put any plan together to address the issues or a way to navigate the case for me clearly.

I felt like I was just pushed across the legal system with him along with me.

I am unsure if it was his abilities as a lawyer or lack of interest in my case but either way I felt alone.

I think the worst thing that he did was when my case was almost over and I called to understand how mediation would go for my case with him, he became agitated and short and verbally told me I find another lawyer a few questions in.

I am writing this review because I just want to share my real experience and make others aware of my personal experience and its not to criticize
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your feedback. Family law matters are personal and often stressful, and we strive to provide both legal guidance and support during these difficult times. We are sorry to hear that you felt unsupported during your case. Our goal is always to provide clear communication and robust representation. As we cannot address specific details publicly due to confidentiality rules, we would have welcomed the opportunity to discuss these concerns with you directly during the representation period.

I just want to say how truly grateful I am to Attorney Lam! When I decided to get a divorce, I was completely lost, but I was so fortunate to have found him. He understood my needs and helped me with everything I asked for. He completely understood my situation and circumstances. During this whole legal process, I didn’t have to worry much — I just needed to provide the documents he asked for. I entrusted my and my daughter’s lives entirely to him. In the end, we won the case, and I no longer have to worry about the days ahead. I sincerely thank Attorney Lam for reaching out to help me and for offering such reasonable fees, allowing me to live a more stable life. If you ever need legal help, don’t hesitate — Attorney Lam is absolutely trustworthy and dependable!

Don't go here just because the price is low, then find another lawyer and find out they were very unprofessional. Your response to your own supposed reasons without understanding the reasons is unsubstantiated. Clearly, your title is taking the judge's decision before he or she has even rendered a verdict. Remember, you are a lawyer, not a judge. A professional lawyer wouldn't make such a mistake; it's extremely unethical. If you want to make the text messages public, everyone will understand.
